codeReview.summary: The feature meets VAL-TYPES-003: `PlanStatus` now composes the shared `CommonTaskStatus` base plus the plan-only `done` state, `normalizePlanStatus` accepts the unified set, and the `normalizePlanSteps`/`buildRunPlan` logic remains behaviorally unchanged in both the reviewed diff and current code. I found no blocking correctness issues.

issues.description: Reviewed commit `c08c3e6` bundled an unrelated version bump here and in `packages/extension/manifest.json` / `packages/extension/manifest.firefox.json` even though the feature scope was limited to shared plan-type refactoring. This does not break VAL-TYPES-003, but it widens the change set and makes the feature harder to audit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `refactor-worker` skill's Final Validation step requires `npm run test:integration` and `npm run lint`, but this worker reported `followedProcedure: true` after recording only typecheck, unit tests, build, and line-count checks. Either the skill is stricter than the mission expects, or workers need to record deviations more accurately.

The shared architecture library is stale for `packages/shared/src`: it still documents monolithic `orchestrator.ts` and `providers.ts`, while the repo now uses split modules such as `orchestrator-types.ts`, `task-status-helpers.ts`, `plan-builders.ts`, `validation-helpers.ts`, and `provider-instance.ts`. Future workers will be pointed at outdated file names and module boundaries.

summary: Pass. The plan-type refactor preserves the required behavior and aligns statuses with the shared common-task base, with one non-blocking scoping issue: the reviewed commit also included unrelated version bumps. I also noted shared-state gaps in the refactor-worker skill validation instructions and the stale architecture library entry for shared-module layout.
